title: Living in Boulder vlog 3 - 26 - 2017: 40 kilometers in background

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder late at night after a long work day spent measuring distances/circles in Google Earth for a mapping project, estimating it will take three days total. He also finished listening to 'Dark Money' by Jane Mayer and wants to listen to it repeatedly, make a webpage about it, and use it to attract Patreon donors.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Worth listening to multiple times; wants to build a webpage around it and tie it to Patreon fundraising
Tedious but worth it — once set up, subsequent use is easy
Using them as design reference examples for his own new website

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-Loc, for March 26, 2017.
Today, I think I got up, I was working until 3 yesterday morning.
And then I got up at like noon.
So, I mean, it was like 11, so, yeah, you know, it was like 8 hours or whatever.
Um, and right now it's, uh, 2 a.m., so, yeah, I gotta go to bed now.
Um, so today was my first day doing all, doing work, it was all day doing, uh, the distances for my circles, areas.
And, uh, yeah, I was kind of organized it wrong.
I had them separately, and I should have had them grouped together.
And then you separate them after you know what you have.
But, um, but, yeah, I'm thinking, I don't know, probably three days total on this one.
But it's worth it, because, you know, once I built the Google Earth part, then it's just clicking on it.
Yeah, the rest is downhill, really cool.
Fucking gummy fun.
Yeah, it's worth it.
Um, tomorrow I'll transfer my money over, get out of debt, that'll be fine.
Um, oh, yeah, I'll try to clean my, or fix my curtain tomorrow, and take photos and upload them.
Yeah.
Um, today I was listening, oh, I finished that book, Dark Money, by Jane Mayer.
Um, but I'm hooked on it, and I want to listen to it again.
I think I want to listen to it over and over again.
Probably have to listen to it, like, three or five times to get the same out of it that you would if you were to read it, like, really slowly.
Yeah, because I want to make a webpage about it.
I want to use it as a way to help me get donors for my Patreon page.
Kind of tie it all together, you know.
Um, yeah, so, yeah, I guess I have three more weeks, so, thank God.
To get done with this circles thing, though, would make me feel like I accomplished something.
Um, yeah, so I got up my roommate again and used to give me a pizza.
I didn't have to go to the store today.
Um, and, uh, so I was listening to that lady, Jane Mayer, also, you know.
And then she'd mention something, like the Cato Institute or the Olin Foundation or the Libertarian website.
When I, when I, um, and so then I would, you know, segue and go check that stuff out.
But I want to, um, when I, like, give examples about why my website designs better than everybody else's,
I'm going to use these websites as examples.
The Politiconomy website, it's like Breitbart News and, oh, that's got a silly design.
And then, um, who else?
The Libertarian website.
Children's, children, child's play.
Um, so, anyways, okay, 316.
I think I've talked one enough because I want to go to Sleepy Pie.
Okay, ciao.
